records.ety: [F., prob. earlier meaning a dispute, orig. in the game of mall (F. mail), fr. LGr. <?/ the game of mall, fr Pers chaugān club or bat; or possibly ultimated fr. L. ciccus a trible.]

defs: The use of artful subterfuge, designed to draw away attention from the merits of a case or question; -- specifically applied to legal proceedings; trickery; chicanery; caviling; sophistry.
quotes: To shuffle from them by chicane.
To cut short this chicane, I propound it fairly to your own conscience.

defs: In bridge, the holding of a hand without trumps, or the hand itself. It counts as simple honors.
To use shifts, cavils, or artifices.
